mission.title: Install and Configure Weaviate
mission.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database that combines vector search with structured filtering, making it ideal for applications like semantic search and recommendation engines. It offers cloud-native features such as multi-tenancy and RBAC, but requires careful resource management for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Weaviate CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Weaviate command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Weaviate deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/releases/download/v1.36.0/weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/weaviate

```

> This command downloads the Weaviate CLI and makes it executable.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate/releases/download/v1.36.0/weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv weaviate-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/weaviate

steps.title: Create a Weaviate cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Weaviate cluster on a managed Kubernetes service with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create weaviate-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30.0

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and configured.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Weaviate cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials weaviate-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify the nodes in the cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Weaviate cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Weaviate deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/master/deploy/k8s/weaviate-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/master/deploy/k8s/weaviate-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for Weaviate.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Weaviate cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Weaviate cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete weaviate-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This command deletes the cluster without prompting for confirmation.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Weaviate cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades the Weaviate deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp <your-backup> <namespace>/<pod-name>:/path/to/backup

gcloud container clusters upgrade weaviate-cluster --cluster-version=1.36.0 --zone=us-central1-a

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Weaviate'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Weaviate service is not running or the network policy is blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Weaviate pods with 'kubectl get pods' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources available for the Weaviate pod.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests in the Weaviate deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error suggests that the Weaviate service is not reachable, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure that the correct ports are exposed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database connection failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the database service is not running or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the database service logs and ensure the connection settings are correct.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vectorization models and improved RBAC features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old vectorization API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.35.0, ensure to update your API calls to the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in the 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
